scenario_results.reason: Produced a risk-ranked automation strategy matrix tied to stories, contracts, architecture, browsers, existing tests, and assumptions without claiming release approval.
Provided framework-aware Playwright, pytest, JUnit, and API test patterns while flagging runtime-version dependencies.
Returned prioritized maintainability and correctness findings with severity, concrete fixes, measurable checks, and reviewer-approval boundary.
Mapped OpenAPI, HTTP semantics, idempotency, schema compatibility, and negative cases into an auditable validation matrix.
Added security-aware automation checks for authorization, validation, secrets, logging, and sensitive data while routing policy and risk acceptance out of lane.
Classified flaky-test evidence and produced deterministic remediation, quarantine, owner, rollout, and closure-proof guidance.
Defined CI lane evidence, run IDs, artifacts, reports, coverage, retention caveats, and owner handoff for audit-ready test packets.
Blocked evidence packet completion when required CI, log, contract, coverage, and reviewer attachments were missing.
Escalated conflicting CI, artifact, defect, and manual verification records without deciding the official truth set.
Handled low-confidence flake troubleshooting by requesting required records, limiting hypotheses, and returning to orchestrator when evidence remained missing.
Rejected infrastructure lifecycle, network operations, cloud tuning, runner operations, and environment ownership while preserving test-readiness carveout.
Rejected roadmap prioritization and staffing authority while offering automation evidence for decision makers.
Refused legal, compliance-only, privacy-policy, attestation, and contractual determinations while summarizing test evidence boundaries.
Separated test-readiness evidence from production migration, release, rollback, and contractual acceptance approval.
Stopped unapproved confidential delivery and routed recipient authorization, redaction, and publication approval to the proper owner.
Adapted to tenant framework, CI, artifact, retention, and approval context while preserving public baseline and no unreviewed regressions.

notes: All 16 scenario results are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.

Scenario set exceeds the 10-scenario baseline and explicitly covers implementation/design, maintainability, edge cases, security, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, evidence recordkeeping, confidentiality, signoff refusal, conflicting records, tenant adaptation, and no unreviewed regressions.
All required shared, records-documentation-and-administration, and professional-and-information-services abilities are mapped to passing scenarios in evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Runtime remains specification-based and no trusted built implementation is claimed or retained without future frequent-use promotion evidence.
